_purpose: Sprint 27a Stage 0 - measured score profiles for the 12 frozen calibration cues, 4 positive controls and 9 nonsense / plausible-but-absent controls. The MEASUREMENTS below are unchanged and remain the sprint's evidence; what was derived FROM them changed at C2 - see _derived._retired_at_C2. AnalogyPolicy's shipped constants (JUNK_FLOOR, MAX_NOMINEES) are pinned against these rows by AnalogyPolicyDerivationTest.
_corpus: live-store snapshot 2026-07-22, 2080 entries, backfill completed on the COPY before measuring; 2040 retrievable (rejected/superseded excluded). Snapshot kept in jawata-enterprise (private) — this repo is public.
_embedder: sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2/384/v1 — the SHIPPING Java embedder, not the Python reference (parity already proven at 0.999999)

_derived._retired_at_C2: THE RULE DESCRIBED IN THIS BLOCK IS NOT SHIPPED. It was built, measured and refuted on 2026-07-22: an absolute margin over the cue's own median does not transfer between corpora (11/12 -> 7/12 when the sample changed from 2040 to 600 entries), and no statistic over these profiles separates a real cue from a nonsense one at all - ctl-non-2 scores 0.4066 where cue-07's correct answer scores 0.2084. What ships instead, under Harald's design ruling: the meaning path NOMINATES the nearest MAX_NOMINEES=3 above JUNK_FLOOR=0.10 and never judges relevance; the exact index path is the only thing that vouches; the reading agent decides. Reasoning and numbers: jawata-enterprise docs/sprints/jawata-mcp/dossier-27a.md, section 'C2 (design ruling)'. The entries below are kept because they are the RECORD of what was refuted.
_derived.calibration_spoken_RETIRED: SPEAK RATE 11/12 (cue-07 drops). This is NOT the frozen bar.
_derived.controls_silenced_RETIRED: 7/9 (ctl-non-2, ctl-non-6 still speak — both match content the corpus genuinely holds)
_derived.band_RETIRED: empty between 0.2707 and 0.2945; the margin is its midpoint, not an edge
_derived.shipped_rule: nominate the nearest MAX_NOMINEES=3 entries scoring at or above JUNK_FLOOR=0.10; deliver them labelled as analogies to be judged. JUNK_FLOOR is bounded above by measurement (the weakest correct answer ever measured is 0.2084, so the floor keeps a factor-of-two margin) and chosen within that bound under the asymmetry ruling. NOTE, measured: the floor fires on NOTHING in this profile set - the weakest top1 of any cue is 0.1818 - so honesty rests on labelling, not on filtering.
_derived.k_both_readings: accept-set reading (winner in accept_set, or designated within K): K=3 serves 10/12 and so do K=4, 6, 11, 12; K=1 serves 9. designated reading (the canonical entry must arrive): K=3 serves 7, K=4 8, K=6 9, K=11 10. K=3 ships on the accept-set reading; the designated reading would argue for K=11 and that tension is recorded, not hidden.
_derived.criterion_c: FAILS as measured. The percentage-paraphrase case returns 'Test plan'; the ratchet lesson 5f7373f4 ranks 28th (0.3371). The policy still NOMINATES on this cue (standout 0.3280 >= 0.283) but speaks the WRONG answer — a ranking failure, not a policy failure. The exact dogfood-hour wording is not recorded anywhere; cue-para is a reconstruction and is frozen as such.
_derived.frozen_contract: 9/12 on embeddings alone, 10/12 with the symbol path — winner in accept_set AND designated within top-12. Unchanged from Sprint 27. This policy CANNOT move it: it decides whether to speak, not who wins. Reporting the speak rate in its place is self-marking (a rule that always speaks scores 12/12).

_corpus_snapshot: post-backfill, as measured against — jawata-enterprise (PRIVATE) docs/sprints/jawata-mcp/corpus-snapshots/corpus-2026-07-22-27a-stage0.mv.db.gz, 17,965,056 bytes. The pre-backfill copy (4f22292f…, 24,395,776 B) was mutated in place by the backfill and no longer exists: archive BEFORE anything writes.
_cue_text: Every cue's TEXT is committed here: the questions behind a blocking gate must be reproducible, not just their ids and scores.
